page: Amazon Web Services m6idn.large is a general-purpose instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz. The instance is configured with 2 vCPUs, consisting of 1 physical core supporting hyper-threading with 2 threads per core. It is equipped with 8.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is bundled as a 118 GB local NVMe SSD instance store volume, and the instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations indicate average performance in single-core workloads, with a stress-ng single-core CPU score of 1881.78 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1619.0. However, multi-core and memory-intensive benchmarks show limitations, with stress-ng multi-core scoring 1926.19 and memory read-only large block bandwidth measured at 14.56 GB/sec, both falling into the lower performance tiers. Cryptographic and compression tasks perform moderately, as evidenced by a single-threaded gzip compression speed of 28.12 GB/sec.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the m6idn.large offers a balanced entry-level resource profile for workloads requiring dedicated CPU allocation and local NVMe storage without the overhead of larger instances. Given its moderate single-core performance and lower multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities, this instance is best suited for light general-purpose workloads, basic web serving, single-threaded applications, and development or testing environments that benefit from fast local temporary storage.

Performance benchmarks show average single-core capabilities, such as a Geekbench single-core score of 1619.0, but reveal lower multi-core and memory bandwidth efficiency. This instance is qualitatively cost-effective for entry-level workloads requiring dedicated CPU resources and fast local storage, making it suitable for light web serving, single-threaded processing, and development environments.

tagline: An entry-level general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processing and integrated local NVMe SSD storage for balanced workloads.

Equipped with 8.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 118 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Delivers 3 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via the AWS Nitro hypervisor
